--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KNQXXF5W7G4JP73C6ZCMKYKX
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ff8b80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Drill-in/drill-out commands + Enter for drill-in, Space for inspect
+---
+## What
+
+Add explicit drill-in and drill-out commands to complement the three-rule beam search. Arrow keys alone handle ordinary nav; these commands give the user access to zone-level focus (drill-out) and into-zone descent (drill-in), matching the nested zone model.
+
+Key-chord change: **Inspect moves from Enter to Space**, freeing Enter to mean drill-in / activate.
+
+### Commands
+
+**`nav.drill_in`** — bound to **Enter** (CUA) and **l** or **Enter** (vim, reusing existing bindings where compatible)
+- On a `Zone`: focus the zone's `last_focused` if still registered, else the zone's first child entry (via edge command `First` scoped to the zone's children).
+- On a `Leaf` with an edit affordance: enter inline edit (existing `inspector.edit` / card-name edit).
+- On a `Leaf` without an edit affordance: no-op.
+
+**`nav.drill_out`** — bound to **Escape** (CUA) and **h** or **Escape** (vim)
+- In inline edit mode: exit edit (existing behavior — field editor's `onCancel` / `inspector.exitEdit`).
+- On a `Leaf`: focus its `parent_zone` (the enclosing Zone) via `setFocus`. If `parent_zone == None`, fall through to the next command (usually `app.dismiss`).
+- On a `Zone`: focus *its* `parent_zone` (if any). If `parent_zone == None`, fall through to `app.dismiss`.
+- The fall-through to dismiss preserves the existing Escape chain: closes topmost modal layer (dialog → inspector) when at layer root.
+
+**`ui.inspect`** — rebind from **Enter → Space** (CUA). Vim binding stays as it is (or picks a sensible vim key; default candidates: `o` or `<Space>`).
+- Behavior unchanged: opens the inspector panel for the focused entity (by moniker).
+
+### Rust side
+
+`navigate` and its cousins don't change. Drill-in/drill-out are resolved in the state manager:
+
+```rust
+pub fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String> {
+    let focused = self.entries.get(&key)?;
+    if focused.kind != ScopeKind::Zone { return None; }  // leaves handled in React (enter edit)
+    if let Some(last) = &focused.last_focused {
+        if self.entries.contains_key(last) { return self.entries.get(last).map(|e| e.moniker.clone()); }
+    }
+    // Fall back to first child via edge-first within the zone
+    self.children_of_zone(*key).min_by_key(|e| (e.rect.top as i64, e.rect.left as i64)).map(|e| e.moniker.clone())
+}
+
+pub fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String> {
+    let focused = self.entries.get(&key)?;
+    let parent_zone_key = focused.parent_zone.as_ref()?;
+    self.entries.get(parent_zone_key).map(|e| e.moniker.clone())
+}
+```
+
+Returns the new target moniker. Caller updates `focus_by_window`, emits `focus-changed`. If `drill_out` returns `None`, React can fall through to the next command in the chain (close modal, etc.).
+
+Tauri commands:
+- `spatial_drill_in(window: tauri::Window, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String>`
+- `spatial_drill_out(window: tauri::Window, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String>`
+
+### React side
+
+Add a command `nav.drillIn` (bound to Enter) and `nav.drillOut` (bound to Escape) to a new top-level command scope (or to app shell). Both first check for inline-edit mode on the focused entity (existing affordance), then invoke the Tauri command, then fall through via `executeNext` or by returning a sentinel "not handled" — whichever the existing command chain uses.
+
+Sweep existing bindings for Enter/Escape and either:
+- Migrate them if they map to drill-in semantics already (e.g. `inspector.edit` becomes the handler for `nav.drillIn` when focused on an inspector field leaf).
+- Leave alone if they are unrelated.
+
+### Remapping Inspect
+
+Grep the command registry for `keys.cua === "Enter"` usages on `ui.inspect` or similar commands, and change to `"Space"`. Likely locations:
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/*` wherever `ui.inspect` is registered
+- Possibly in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/*` where command defaults live
+
+Document the new binding somewhere visible (a keybinding reference or the `ui.inspect` command description).
+
+### Subtasks
+- [ ] Add Rust `drill_in` / `drill_out` methods on `SpatialRegistry` or state manager
+- [ ] Add Tauri commands `spatial_drill_in` / `spatial_drill_out`
+- [ ] Add React commands `nav.drillIn` (Enter) and `nav.drillOut` (Escape)
+- [ ] Migrate `inspector.edit`/field-row edit handling to integrate with `nav.drillIn` (Enter still edits a field-level leaf)
+- [ ] Rebind `ui.inspect` (and any analogues) to Space (CUA)
+- [ ] Verify Escape chain still closes modals at layer root
+- [ ] Update the keybinding documentation / command descriptions
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Enter on a Zone → drills into its `last_focused` or first child
+- [ ] Enter on a Leaf with edit affordance → enters inline edit (e.g. field value editor, card-name rename)
+- [ ] Enter on a Leaf without edit affordance → no-op (does not open inspector)
+- [ ] Escape on a Leaf → focuses its `parent_zone`
+- [ ] Escape on a Zone → focuses its `parent_zone` if any
+- [ ] Escape at layer root falls through to existing dismiss / close-modal behavior
+- [ ] Space on any focused entity → opens inspector (`ui.inspect` moniker-routed)
+- [ ] `cargo test` and `pnpm vitest run` pass
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Zone with live `last_focused` returns that entry's moniker
+- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Zone whose `last_focused` is stale returns first child's moniker
+- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Leaf returns `None`
+- [ ] Rust: `drill_out` on a Leaf returns the `parent_zone` entry's moniker
+- [ ] Rust: `drill_out` on a Zone returns its `parent_zone` entry's moniker, or None at layer root
+- [ ] React: Enter key on a focused zone invokes `spatial_drill_in` and `setFocus` with the returned moniker
+- [ ] React: Escape on a focused leaf invokes `spatial_drill_out`; when return is null, falls through to dismiss
+- [ ] React: Space key triggers `ui.inspect` for the focused entity, opening the inspector panel
+- [ ] React: Enter on a task card edit-rename leaf enters inline rename (not inspector)
+- [ ] Run `cargo test` and `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Drill-in/drill-out commands + Enter for drill-in, Space for inspect

## What

Add explicit drill-in and drill-out commands to complement the three-rule beam search. Arrow keys alone handle ordinary nav; these commands give the user access to zone-level focus (drill-out) and into-zone descent (drill-in), matching the nested zone model.

Key-chord change: **Inspect moves from Enter to Space**, freeing Enter to mean drill-in / activate.

### Commands

**`nav.drill_in`** — bound to **Enter** (CUA) and **l** or **Enter** (vim, reusing existing bindings where compatible)
- On a `Zone`: focus the zone's `last_focused` if still registered, else the zone's first child entry (via edge command `First` scoped to the zone's children).
- On a `Leaf` with an edit affordance: enter inline edit (existing `inspector.edit` / card-name edit).
- On a `Leaf` without an edit affordance: no-op.

**`nav.drill_out`** — bound to **Escape** (CUA) and **h** or **Escape** (vim)
- In inline edit mode: exit edit (existing behavior — field editor's `onCancel` / `inspector.exitEdit`).
- On a `Leaf`: focus its `parent_zone` (the enclosing Zone) via `setFocus`. If `parent_zone == None`, fall through to the next command (usually `app.dismiss`).
- On a `Zone`: focus *its* `parent_zone` (if any). If `parent_zone == None`, fall through to `app.dismiss`.
- The fall-through to dismiss preserves the existing Escape chain: closes topmost modal layer (dialog → inspector) when at layer root.

**`ui.inspect`** — rebind from **Enter → Space** (CUA). Vim binding stays as it is (or picks a sensible vim key; default candidates: `o` or `<Space>`).
- Behavior unchanged: opens the inspector panel for the focused entity (by moniker).

### Rust side

`navigate` and its cousins don't change. Drill-in/drill-out are resolved in the state manager:

```rust
pub fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String> {
    let focused = self.entries.get(&key)?;
    if focused.kind != ScopeKind::Zone { return None; }  // leaves handled in React (enter edit)
    if let Some(last) = &focused.last_focused {
        if self.entries.contains_key(last) { return self.entries.get(last).map(|e| e.moniker.clone()); }
    }
    // Fall back to first child via edge-first within the zone
    self.children_of_zone(*key).min_by_key(|e| (e.rect.top as i64, e.rect.left as i64)).map(|e| e.moniker.clone())
}

pub fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String> {
    let focused = self.entries.get(&key)?;
    let parent_zone_key = focused.parent_zone.as_ref()?;
    self.entries.get(parent_zone_key).map(|e| e.moniker.clone())
}
```

Returns the new target moniker. Caller updates `focus_by_window`, emits `focus-changed`. If `drill_out` returns `None`, React can fall through to the next command in the chain (close modal, etc.).

Tauri commands:
- `spatial_drill_in(window: tauri::Window, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String>`
- `spatial_drill_out(window: tauri::Window, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<String>`

### React side

Add a command `nav.drillIn` (bound to Enter) and `nav.drillOut` (bound to Escape) to a new top-level command scope (or to app shell). Both first check for inline-edit mode on the focused entity (existing affordance), then invoke the Tauri command, then fall through via `executeNext` or by returning a sentinel "not handled" — whichever the existing command chain uses.

Sweep existing bindings for Enter/Escape and either:
- Migrate them if they map to drill-in semantics already (e.g. `inspector.edit` becomes the handler for `nav.drillIn` when focused on an inspector field leaf).
- Leave alone if they are unrelated.

### Remapping Inspect

Grep the command registry for `keys.cua === "Enter"` usages on `ui.inspect` or similar commands, and change to `"Space"`. Likely locations:
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/*` wherever `ui.inspect` is registered
- Possibly in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/*` where command defaults live

Document the new binding somewhere visible (a keybinding reference or the `ui.inspect` command description).

### Subtasks
- [ ] Add Rust `drill_in` / `drill_out` methods on `SpatialRegistry` or state manager
- [ ] Add Tauri commands `spatial_drill_in` / `spatial_drill_out`
- [ ] Add React commands `nav.drillIn` (Enter) and `nav.drillOut` (Escape)
- [ ] Migrate `inspector.edit`/field-row edit handling to integrate with `nav.drillIn` (Enter still edits a field-level leaf)
- [ ] Rebind `ui.inspect` (and any analogues) to Space (CUA)
- [ ] Verify Escape chain still closes modals at layer root
- [ ] Update the keybinding documentation / command descriptions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enter on a Zone → drills into its `last_focused` or first child
- [ ] Enter on a Leaf with edit affordance → enters inline edit (e.g. field value editor, card-name rename)
- [ ] Enter on a Leaf without edit affordance → no-op (does not open inspector)
- [ ] Escape on a Leaf → focuses its `parent_zone`
- [ ] Escape on a Zone → focuses its `parent_zone` if any
- [ ] Escape at layer root falls through to existing dismiss / close-modal behavior
- [ ] Space on any focused entity → opens inspector (`ui.inspect` moniker-routed)
- [ ] `cargo test` and `pnpm vitest run` pass

## Tests
- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Zone with live `last_focused` returns that entry's moniker
- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Zone whose `last_focused` is stale returns first child's moniker
- [ ] Rust: `drill_in` on a Leaf returns `None`
- [ ] Rust: `drill_out` on a Leaf returns the `parent_zone` entry's moniker
- [ ] Rust: `drill_out` on a Zone returns its `parent_zone` entry's moniker, or None at layer root
- [ ] React: Enter key on a focused zone invokes `spatial_drill_in` and `setFocus` with the returned moniker
- [ ] React: Escape on a focused leaf invokes `spatial_drill_out`; when return is null, falls through to dismiss
- [ ] React: Space key triggers `ui.inspect` for the focused entity, opening the inspector panel
- [ ] React: Enter on a task card edit-rename leaf enters inline rename (not inspector)
- [ ] Run `cargo test` and `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
